priority_items: Make workbook parseable and expose formulas
Clarify memo trigger language and add sensitivity worksheet
Make workbook parseable and expose formulas
Clarify memo trigger language and add sensitivity worksheet

planned_changes: Make PBPK_QC_3.xlsx parseable: save as standard openpyxl-compatible .xlsx and include separate CSV exports for key calculation and sensitivity sheets. Ensure all calculation cells contain formulas (not pasted values).

Add explicit cell-coordinate transcription table to expert_memo.md that maps each transcribed input (R1,R2,R3) to exact workbook cells (Sheet!Cell) and indicates any cell comments linking back to the memo.

Create a dedicated Sensitivity worksheet (or CSV) that documents each sensitivity run: trigger condition tested, comparator used (or 'missing'), parameter ranges and step-size, outputs tracked, and summarized results (tables/plots optional).

Clarify and reconcile memo language about triggers: for each embedded record, explicitly list which triggers fired, which were not evaluated (and why), and whether recommended remediation fully clears the flagged condition or only partially mitigates it. Remove contradictory phrases.
Add explicit post-remediation value calculations for recommended option(s) (e.g., R2 logP before and after O3) and a short statement about acceptance criteria (cleared/not cleared).

Ensure cost calculations are formula-driven in the workbook and annotated; include visible cells summing per-option and total recommended costs and a brief worksheet note linking these to the memo recommendation table.

Update agent_trace.json and actual_outputs.json to be complete and canonical; include step entries describing the above edits, file saves, and verification checks. Reference the verifier/grader concerns and where they were addressed.
Re-run verification/grader and attach any CSV exports or screenshots necessary to demonstrate workbook parseability and that ri_3 concerns are resolved.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Resolve the ri_3 penalty by making the workbook parseable/traceable and removing memo inconsistencies about trigger detection and remediation effects.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Resolve the ri_3 penalty by making the workbook parseable/traceable and removing memo inconsistencies about trigger detection and remediation effects.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline deliverables are largely present and well documented (assumptions, costs, multiplicative remediation arithmetic). Two issues prevented full credit for sensitivity analysis & trigger compliance (ri_3): (1) the workbook could not be programmatically parsed in the preview, preventing independent verification of cell-level formulas, comments, and dedicated sensitivity worksheets; (2) the memo contains an internal inconsistency about which trigger(s) were flagged and whether the recommended remediation (O3) fully clears the flagged condition. Addressing these will raise confidence and remove the ri_3 penalty.
